title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams releases 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack with beers inspired by Boston sports stars White, Crochet, Edelman, and Chara.
The pack features beers: White Ale, Crochet's Pale Ale, Julian Edel-Brau Lager, and Big Z Pilsner, each themed after an athlete.
A public launch party at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston with athlete appearances is set for April 4; 12-pack available in stores.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray to run his 54th Boston Marathon with his children Max, Luke, and Elle McGillivray.
Retired astronaut Sunita Williams will join the McGillivray family in the marathon, running with the Finish Strong Foundation.
McGillivray's participation honors late ex-wife Susan Hurley, with family members raising funds for various charitable causes.

title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected before halftime in the Celtics' loss to San Antonio, sparking criticism from teammates.
Jayson Tatum and Derrick White defended Brown, questioning the referee's decision to issue a second technical foul.
Jaylen Brown has been vocal about officiating issues and was previously fined for comments on missed calls.

title: He’s running Boston to honor of his mother’s adventurous spirit
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: James Hebert, 62, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his adventurous mother, Janice.
Hebert will wear a shirt resembling lilacs, honoring his mother's love for the flower and adventurous spirit.
Janice, a New Englander who passed in 2023, enjoyed hang-gliding, skydiving, and ziplining during her life.

title: Authorities look into possible vandalism of expensive geotubes on Nantucket
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Authorities investigate deliberate vandalism of geotubes on Sconset Bluff, cutting them open and leaking sand.
A private investigator noted clean, linear cuts on geotubes consistent with willful vandalism, not accidental or natural forces.
The Sconset Beach Preservation Fund offers a $10,000 reward for information leading to an arrest in the vandalism case.

title: ICE detains Marlborough 14-year-old, apparently as ‘hostage’ to target her father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rep. Lori Trahan claims ICE detained a 14-year-old from Marlborough as leverage against her father for deportation.
DHS refutes Trahan's claims, stating the girl was rescued from suspected gang members affiliated with Primeiro Comando da Capital.
The girl was placed in Health and Human Services custody, pending identification of legal guardians, as she had no guardians with her.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Easthampton resident Natalie Graves, 34, and her aunt were killed while hiking in Utah on March 4.
The suspect in the Utah triple homicide allegedly killed a woman earlier and stole a vehicle to flee.
A fundraiser launched for Graves' husband has raised over $38,000, nearing its financial goal.

title: Here’s what to know about the new $1 billion Gillette headquarters planned for Fort Point
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Procter & Gamble is investing $1 billion in a new Gillette headquarters at 232 A St. in South Boston.
The new site will house the Grooming Headquarters and Technical Innovation Center, replacing the current facility on 1 Gillette Park.
Blade and razor manufacturing will move to a 200,000-square-foot facility in Andover, impacting hundreds of employees.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed Dre'Mont Jones, who previously played for the Ravens, Titans, and Seahawks, to a three-year deal.
Jones recorded 15 quarterback hits in nine games with Baltimore, excelling in pass-rushing roles last season.
At 6-foot-3, 281 pounds, Jones offers versatility on the defensive line, playing multiple positions including defensive end and tackle.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston Harbor will close on March 29 due to 'economic challenges.'
The closure affects 56 workers who unionized less than a month ago; the union calls the shutdown retaliatory.
UNITE HERE Local 26 urges Encore to employ affected workers; Seamark denies the closure is linked to unionization.
